assertions.description: AudioContext is exposed as a constructor.
A new AudioContext exposes a string state — the IDL enum AudioContextState.
AudioContext fires statechange events as the state machine transitions.
A freshly-constructed AudioContext is not in the terminal `closed` state.
AudioContext.suspend() returns a Promise — used in tandem with the interrupted state for explicit user-requested pauses.
assertions.test: (()=>{const ctx=new AudioContext();const p=ctx.suspend();const ok=p instanceof Promise;p.finally(()=>ctx.close());return ok;})()

assertions.description: AudioContext.resume() returns a Promise — needed to recover after an `interrupted` transition.
assertions.test: (()=>{const ctx=new AudioContext();const p=ctx.resume();const ok=p instanceof Promise;p.finally(()=>ctx.close());return ok;})()
